content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "project-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 72,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 52,
    "interaction_quality": 68,
    "ai_integration": 74,
    "mobile_first": 45,
    "performance": 62,
    "typescript_quality": 65,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 48,
    "accessibility": 35,
    "security": 60,
    "differentiation": 70,
    "enterprise_readiness": 50
  },
  "overall_score": 58,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "CopilotPanel chat messages lack ARIA live region and keyboard navigation",
      "file": "src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx",
      "description": "The chat message list has no aria-live region so screen readers never announce new AI responses. Messages are not keyboard navigable. The input textarea lacks an accessible label. This violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards for accessibility.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Find the scrollable message container div (the one that renders AgentMessage components). BEFORE: The message list div has no ARIA attributes and the textarea input has no aria-label. AFTER: Add aria-live='polite' and role='log' and aria-label='AI conversation messages' to the message list container div. Add aria-label='Message to AI copilot' to the AgentMentionInput's parent or pass it as a prop. Ensure the message container has tabIndex={0} so it is keyboard focusable. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "FloatingAIButton swallows refetch errors silently on click",
      "file": "src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx",
      "description": "When the AI insights query is in error state, clicking the button calls refetch() inside a try/catch that shows a toast but then immediately calls openCopilot() regardless of whether refetch succeeded. The catch block calls toast.error but the openCopilot still fires, leading users into a copilot with no context. The error state also lacks a retry-only path.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: Find the onClick handler for the isError case. BEFORE: The handler is `async () => { try { await refetch(); } catch { toast.error('Unable to load AI insights. Please try again.'); } openCopilot(); }` which always calls openCopilot even on failure. AFTER: Change to `async () => { try { await refetch(); openCopilot(); } catch { toast.error('Unable to load AI insights. Please try again.'); } }` so openCopilot only runs on successful refetch. Also add aria-live='polite' to the error badge span so screen readers announce the error state. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "CopilotPanel fixed width 360px does not adapt below 375px viewport",
      "file": "src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx",
      "description": "The AIContextPanel and CopilotPanel both use a fixed width of 360px with maxWidth 85vw. On iPhone SE (375px), this leaves only 15px for the scrim/overlay edge, and the close button may be partially offscreen. The suggested prompts grid does not wrap on narrow screens. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA, every page must be usable at 375px width.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Find the root motion.aside element that has style width: '360px', maxWidth: '85vw'. BEFORE: width is '360px' and maxWidth is '85vw'. On 375px screens this is 318px which is acceptable but the internal content (suggested prompt cards with fixed widths, the agent mention input) may overflow. AFTER: Change width to '100vw' and maxWidth to '400px' for screens above 768px and '100vw' for below (use a CSS media query via a style calculation or simply set width to 'min(400px, 100vw)' and remove the separate maxWidth). Also ensure the suggested prompt buttons in CONTEXT_PROMPTS rendering use flexWrap: 'wrap' and that each prompt card has minWidth: 0 to prevent horizontal overflow. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "AI insights query has no real-time subscription for live updates",
      "file": "src/hooks/queries.ts",
      "description": "The useAIInsights hook (referenced by FloatingAIButton and Dashboard) fetches AI insights once via React Query but never subscribes to Supabase Realtime changes on the ai_insights table. When another user or a background edge function creates new insights, the current user sees stale data until they manually refresh. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Dashboard criterion 9, metrics should update live.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/queries.ts, find the useAIInsights hook (or the query function that fetches from the ai_insights table). BEFORE: The hook uses useQuery with a queryKey like ['ai-insights', projectId] but has no Supabase Realtime subscription to invalidate or refetch when new rows are inserted into ai_insights. AFTER: Add a useEffect inside useAIInsights (or create a companion hook) that subscribes to supabase.channel(`ai_insights:${projectId}`).on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'ai_insights', filter: `project_id=eq.${projectId}` }, () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['ai-insights', projectId] })). Make sure to call supabase.removeChannel on cleanup. Import supabase from '../lib/supabase' and useQueryClient from '@tanstack/react-query'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "AgentMentionInput has no loading/disabled visual feedback when sending",
      "file": "src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx",
      "description": "When disabled prop is true (during message send), the textarea and send button have no visual distinction from the enabled state. The send button does not show a spinner or change opacity. Users can still focus and type in the textarea even when disabled is true because the disabled prop is not passed to the textarea element. This creates confusion about whether the message was sent.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: Find the textarea element and the send button. BEFORE: The textarea does not have a disabled attribute even though the component accepts a disabled prop. The send button does not visually change when disabled. AFTER: Add disabled={disabled} to the textarea element. Add opacity: disabled ? 0.5 : 1 and cursor: disabled ? 'not-allowed' : 'text' to the textarea style. For the send button, when disabled is true, set opacity to 0.5 and cursor to 'not-allowed'. Also add aria-disabled={disabled} to both the textarea and the send button for screen reader support. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "The AI Copilot module is architecturally ambitious with multi-agent orchestration, generative UI blocks, and specialist agent routing — features no competitor ships today. Procore's AI Assist is a simple search chatbot. Autodesk Construction IQ does risk prediction but has no conversational interface. However, SiteSync's implementation has gaps in accessibility, mobile responsiveness, and real-time data freshness that would fail enterprise procurement checklists. The generative UI components (charts, approval cards, cost breakdowns) are a genuine differentiator if they work reliably end-to-end.",
  "summary": "The Project Intelligence module has strong architectural bones — multi-agent chat, generative UI rendering, context-aware prompts, and a rich component library. However, it falls short on fundamentals that enterprise buyers test first: accessibility is poor (no ARIA live regions on the chat, no keyboard navigation), mobile responsiveness breaks below 400px, real-time subscriptions are missing so insights go stale, and error handling has a logic bug in the floating button that opens the copilot even when data fails to load. The five fixes target these gaps: ARIA attributes for screen readers, error flow logic, mobile viewport handling, Realtime subscription wiring, and disabled state visual feedback. These are surgical fixes that do not change architecture but raise the module from 'impressive demo' to 'passes QA audit' territory."
}
```
